jokes.text: It's just the more you keep mentioning that it's a gift, the less and less it feels like a gift.
jokes.setup: Johnny keeps emphasizing that the wedding food is his 'gift' to David
jokes.punchline: David points out the contradiction - over-emphasizing generosity negates the generosity
jokes.explanation: Perfect David Rose character moment - neurotic but astute observation about social dynamics, delivered with his characteristic directness

jokes.text: Look at you, John, doling out gift after gift this morning like the Turkish Saint Nikolaas.
jokes.setup: Johnny has just inadvertently invited Twyla to the wedding
jokes.punchline: Moira's overwrought comparison to Turkish Saint Nicholas for minor acts of generosity
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - unnecessarily ornate language for mundane situations, with the specific 'Turkish' detail being perfectly random

jokes.text: David, I didn't know she wasn't invited to the wedding. / Well, you certainly fixed that problem for yourself.
jokes.setup: Johnny accidentally invited Twyla while trying to be diplomatic
jokes.punchline: David's dry acknowledgment that Johnny created more problems while solving one
jokes.explanation: Sharp character interplay showing David's exasperation with his father's well-meaning blunders

jokes.text: Tippy always had his fingers in a few fires.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Tippy Bernstein wanting a meeting
jokes.punchline: Moira mangles the idiom 'fingers in many pies' with 'irons in the fire'
jokes.explanation: Typical Moira malapropism - attempts sophisticated language but muddles common idioms

jokes.text: Oh, you're looking so natural and peppy. / A bit of a rough night actually. / Well, you're fooling everyone, dear.
jokes.setup: Moira compliments Alexis on looking good
jokes.punchline: Alexis admits she looks terrible, Moira doubles down on the obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Standard mother-daughter dynamic with Moira's characteristic inability to read situations

jokes.text: It's printed right here on the cover of the book that he's handing out to all of you.
jokes.setup: Johnny formally introduces himself to new motel staff
jokes.punchline: Roland points out the redundancy since Johnny's name is on the books he's distributing
jokes.explanation: Roland's anti-corporate attitude undermining Johnny's attempts at professionalism

jokes.text: And I'm the boss you're gonna come to when you don't understand why this guy is making you read about VHS tapes.
jokes.setup: Roland introducing himself as co-owner to new staff
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermines Johnny's authority while misunderstanding the business book content
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland moment - completely missing the point while asserting dominance

jokes.text: Not exactly the united front we had discussed, Roland.
jokes.setup: Roland just completely undermined Johnny in front of new employees
jokes.punchline: Johnny's understated response to being sabotaged
jokes.explanation: Johnny's passive-aggressive politeness in the face of Roland's chaos

jokes.text: Ronnie found termite damage in Room Six and apparently the entire foundation is about to collapse.
jokes.setup: Stevie wants to speak privately about motel issues
jokes.punchline: Casually delivers catastrophic news about structural collapse
jokes.explanation: Stevie's characteristic deadpan delivery of terrible news during the worst possible moment

jokes.text: Well, I gave one of my kidneys to my cousin Bruce. Uh, we can see how I run on an empty tank.
jokes.setup: Discussion of how to cover termite repair costs
jokes.punchline: Roland casually mentions organ donation as if it's a business expense, then complains about performance
jokes.explanation: Absurd Roland logic treating major surgery like a casual business transaction

jokes.text: I went to school with a guy who starts 'accidental' fires. I mean, worse comes to worst, we could collect the insurance money.
jokes.setup: Continuing discussion of financial solutions
jokes.punchline: Roland casually suggests insurance fraud with air quotes around 'accidental'
jokes.explanation: Roland's casual criminality delivered with small-town matter-of-factness

jokes.text: because Bruce told me my kidney was garbage.
jokes.setup: Johnny says they're not selling organs
jokes.punchline: Roland reveals his kidney donation was rejected, adding insult to injury
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that makes the previous absurd situation even more pathetic and funny

jokes.text: Take the pills, Vivian. / A 'please' might be nice!
jokes.setup: Watching Sunrise Bay on TV
jokes.punchline: Moira critiques her own character's rudeness as if watching someone else
jokes.explanation: Moira's disconnect from her own past performance, treating herself as a separate entity

jokes.text: Oh, suddenly she's the director.
jokes.setup: Alexis questions plot logic of Sunrise Bay
jokes.punchline: Moira dismisses her daughter's valid criticism with sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Moira's defensive reaction to criticism of her professional work

jokes.text: Well, I feel like your publicist should be there if you're meeting with Tickle Bernstein. / Tippy. It's Tippy
jokes.setup: Alexis wants to accompany Moira to the meeting
jokes.punchline: Gets the producer's name wrong in an unfortunately childish way
jokes.explanation: Simple name confusion that plays into Alexis's sometimes childish perspective

jokes.text: How dare you make me kill my father twice!
jokes.setup: Watching melodramatic soap opera dialogue
jokes.punchline: The inherently ridiculous nature of soap opera plotting
jokes.explanation: Standard soap opera parody highlighting the absurd melodrama of the genre

jokes.text: David would understand. I mean, not at first, but deep down he's not a total monster.
jokes.setup: Johnny considering backing out of paying for David's wedding food
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment suggesting David is mostly monstrous but not totally
jokes.explanation: Perfect father-son dynamic where love comes through insults and qualifications

jokes.text: So we're two minutes late and if this were the actual wedding, the doors would be closed and you would be locked out.
jokes.setup: Arriving at wedding food tasting
jokes.punchline: David immediately catastrophizing minor lateness with extreme wedding etiquette
jokes.explanation: David's perfectionism and anxiety manifesting in wedding planning extremes

jokes.text: Stevie is my Maid of Honour, she's just here to eat.
jokes.setup: David introducing everyone to the caterer
jokes.punchline: Reducing Stevie's presence to pure freeloading
jokes.explanation: David's blunt assessment of Stevie's motivations, which probably isn't wrong

jokes.text: Poor thing. / That's the garnish for the lobster roll.
jokes.setup: Johnny sympathetically comments on what he thinks is a small, sad salad
jokes.punchline: It's revealed to be just garnish, making his comment inappropriately emotional
jokes.explanation: Classic misunderstanding humor where Johnny's empathy is misdirected

jokes.text: Lobster! Ah. Are we inviting The Vanderbilts?
jokes.setup: Learning the appetizer is lobster rolls
jokes.punchline: Johnny's sticker shock expressed through reference to extreme wealth
jokes.explanation: Johnny's attempt at humor to mask his anxiety about wedding costs

jokes.text: if we're not using the lettuce under the lobster roll can we just not make a side salad out of this lettuce?
jokes.setup: Fancy salad options being discussed
jokes.punchline: Johnny desperately trying to repurpose garnish into a full dish to save money
jokes.explanation: Johnny's penny-pinching desperation reaching embarrassing levels

jokes.text: And speaking of, we are adding eight more plates. I was finally allowed to invite some friends from New York, they're a caviar crowd.
jokes.setup: Johnny just learned the high per-plate cost
jokes.punchline: David immediately adds expensive guests who expect expensive food
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and character work - David's obliviousness to his father's financial stress

jokes.text: Oh, you know what they say about tenderloin; always... a risky option. It's gotta be done just, just right.
jokes.setup: Tasting the beef tenderloin
jokes.punchline: Johnny making up food wisdom to mask that he's worried about the cost
jokes.explanation: Johnny trying to sound sophisticated while actually being cheap

jokes.text: Well, you don't want people coming to your wedding and only talking about the food. / That is literally the only thing I want them talking about. What is wrong with you?
jokes.setup: Johnny suggests people shouldn't focus on the food
jokes.punchline: David's horror at this suggestion and questioning his father's sanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect character clash - Johnny's practical concerns vs David's perfectionism, with great escalation

jokes.text: Why not, it's not like we're paying for the samples.
jokes.setup: Being offered more expensive food to sample
jokes.punchline: Johnny's bitter sarcasm about 'free' samples leading to expensive choices
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic moment showing Johnny's understanding of the sales tactic while being powerless to stop it.

jokes.text: Cured that vertigo years ago. I hired a hynotherapist. Then I married her. And then I divorced her. You-are-getting-very... broke.
jokes.setup: Tippy mentioning his vertigo cure
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire relationship saga ending with financial ruin pun on hypnosis
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure and clever wordplay combining hypnosis with divorce financial consequences

jokes.text: You're looking fresh and dewy. / Gave up the drink. Strictly a wine and scotch man now.
jokes.setup: Moira compliments Clifton's appearance
jokes.punchline: Clifton's definition of sobriety is absurdly narrow
jokes.explanation: Perfect alcoholic logic - technically giving up 'drink' while keeping alcohol

jokes.text: Oh, that was an actual court case. My daughter wanted to emancipate and the press couldn't get enough.
jokes.setup: Moira praising what she thinks is Clifton's acting work
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was his real life family dysfunction, not acting
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy misdirection - Hollywood dysfunction being real rather than fictional

jokes.text: I can't wait for the screener.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Moira's acclaimed 'Crows' movie
jokes.punchline: Clifton admits he hasn't seen her successful film
jokes.explanation: Reveals Clifton's insincerity and jealousy - he won't even watch her success

jokes.text: You vomited a demon into a toilet, and then fell in and drowned.
jokes.setup: Discussion of how Moira's character was killed off
jokes.punchline: Absurdly undignified death sequence for a soap opera character
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and specific death scene that perfectly parodies soap opera excess

jokes.text: Well, the writers insisted it was symbolic.
jokes.setup: Alexis describing the ridiculous death scene
jokes.punchline: Moira's attempt to find artistic merit in obvious humiliation
jokes.explanation: Moira's characteristic self-deception about her professional treatment

jokes.text: She had a good run. But let's just say 'someone' reminded the producers that there's only room for one head of surgery at Sunrise General.
jokes.setup: Interview about Moira's departure from the show
jokes.punchline: Barely concealed admission of sabotaging her career with air quotes
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment revealing industry politics and Clifton's pettiness

jokes.text: Adriana had that DUI last year. Popped a breasts, poor dear
jokes.setup: Discussing other cast members making headlines
jokes.punchline: Moira's malapropism turning 'breast implants' into 'popped a breasts'
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira malapropism with unfortunate grammatical construction

jokes.text: Perhaps a little more housework and little less mouse work might do you some good, Alexis.
jokes.setup: Alexis has been doing internet research
jokes.punchline: Moira's dismissive pun on 'mouse' (computer) vs housework
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing Moira's generational divide and dismissiveness

jokes.text: Penelope asked if you wanted to crack the top of the creme brulée and you asked, 'If we break it, do we buy it?'
jokes.setup: David noticing Johnny's awkwardness at the tasting
jokes.punchline: Johnny treating gourmet food tasting like browsing in a gift shop
jokes.explanation: Perfect character observation showing Johnny's fish-out-of-water status with fine dining

jokes.text: Apparently there is some nest egg they set aside for Patrick and his ex-fiancée.
jokes.setup: David explaining why Patrick's parents gave extra money
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that David is benefiting from his fiancé's failed previous engagement
jokes.explanation: David's matter-of-fact delivery of slightly uncomfortable information

jokes.text: You know, we had a sizable nest egg set aside for David's wedding. He was talking about Bali for a while. We were gonna charter some jets. Fly everybody.
jokes.setup: Johnny reflecting on their reduced circumstances
jokes.punchline: Wistful description of the extravagant wedding they can no longer afford
jokes.explanation: Touching moment showing Johnny's grief over their financial loss and what it means for his son

jokes.text: Hey, tell that cute bartender I'd love a sex on the beach. If she has time, I need another scotch.
jokes.setup: Tippy ordering drinks
jokes.punchline: Ambiguous phrasing that could be interpreted as sexual proposition or drink order
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre playing on cocktail name vs sexual proposition

jokes.text: It was even more vicious than Vivian's getting trampled while honeymooning in Pamplona.
jokes.setup: Moira describing her character's previous death
jokes.punchline: Another ridiculous soap opera death involving running of the bulls
jokes.explanation: Builds on the running gag of ridiculous soap deaths with specific cultural reference

jokes.text: Think of it as a compliment. Your star was rising, and I was maybe jealous.
jokes.setup: Moira confronting Clifton about sabotaging her career
jokes.punchline: Clifton's attempt to reframe career sabotage as a backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Clifton's manipulative attempt to minimize his harmful actions

jokes.text: And-and that season that you played your own brother we submitted you for Best Actress and Actor.
jokes.setup: Tippy claiming they submitted Moira for awards every year
jokes.punchline: Reveals the absurd soap plot where she played her own brother
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist soap opera plotting that's somehow believable for the genre

jokes.text: I mean at this point, I'm willing to sell my baseball signed by Geena Davis.
jokes.setup: Roland trying to come up with valuable items to sell
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific celebrity signature that probably isn't very valuable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland randomness - completely unexpected celebrity reference that makes no sense

jokes.text: What if we bought 30 more motels? Right now.
jokes.setup: Discussion of selling the failing second motel
jokes.punchline: Roland's solution is to dramatically increase their problems
jokes.explanation: Roland's backwards logic presented with complete confidence

jokes.text: A wise man once wrote: 'You miss 100% of the tapes you don't play'.
jokes.setup: Roland trying to inspire Johnny with his own business wisdom
jokes.punchline: Mangled version of Wayne Gretzky quote but with 'tapes' instead of 'shots'
jokes.explanation: Clever callback combining famous quote with the VHS business theme

jokes.text: That's just the dedication, Roland. / That's as far as I got, Johnny. I'm sorry, I'm partial to historical fiction.
jokes.setup: Roland quoting from Johnny's book
jokes.punchline: Reveals Roland only read the dedication and has completely wrong genre expectations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland moment showing his complete lack of engagement with Johnny's work

jokes.text: But on the condition that Clifton be written off the show - death of my choosing. And I asked for more money. / And a diamond tennis bracelet.
jokes.setup: Moira describing her negotiation demands
jokes.punchline: Escalating from reasonable to petty revenge to pure material greed
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Moira's vengeful side with perfect absurd specificity

jokes.text: I made them an offer they had to refuse.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking if Moira is doing the reboot
jokes.punchline: Clever twist on Godfather quote - she intentionally made impossible demands
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay on famous movie quote that perfectly captures Moira's strategy and dignity

jokes.text: Okay, how is it that your daughter is also trapped in the cave but has never looked up once. You've been hanging over her for three straight episodes.
jokes.setup: Continuing to watch Sunrise Bay
jokes.punchline: Pointing out ridiculous blocking where character ignores obvious presence above
jokes.explanation: Good observation about soap opera production absurdities and lazy staging
